seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in AG2 Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in AG2 agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AG2, Context Window Management issues for AI agents manifest as agents exceeding token limits during extended multi-agent conversations, leading to truncated context and degraded performance. The framework's enhanced group chat capabilities amplify this problem, as speaker selection and conversation history accumulate rapidly, causing off-topic spiraling and fact drift. AG2's message routing system makes it challenging to implement effective context pruning without breaking conversation flow.

howItManifests.scenario: Long-running group chat with 5+ agents debating complex topics
howItManifests.symptom: Last 20-30 messages become incoherent; agents repeat themselves or reference non-existent prior statements
howItManifests.impact: Group consensus fails on incorrect facts; conversation derails into repetitive loops

howItManifests.scenario: Code execution agent chain with iterative debugging
howItManifests.symptom: Agent ignores recent error messages buried in 100+ message history
howItManifests.impact: Produces harmful or incorrect code outputs due to context truncation

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-agent debate with speaker selection bias
howItManifests.symptom: Dominant speaker's early messages overshadow recent critical corrections
howItManifests.impact: Group chat converges on outdated or wrong information

howItManifests.scenario: Customer support simulation with role rotation
howItManifests.symptom: Agents lose track of customer requirements after 15+ message exchanges
howItManifests.impact: Provides irrelevant responses; conversation satisfaction drops

detection.description: Measures semantic similarity between recent vs early conversation topics

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Pruner
fixes.description: Automatically removes low-relevance messages while preserving key facts

fixes.name: Conversation Summarizer
fixes.description: Condenses conversation history into key facts before each agent turn
fixes.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()

def summarize_context(chat: GroupChat):
    if len(chat.messages) < 15:
        return chat.messages
    
    history = ' '.join([f'{m.sender}: {m.content[:100]}...' for m in chat.messages[-10:]])
    
    summary_prompt = f"""
Summarize key facts and decisions from this conversation in 200 words:
{history}

Key Facts:
"""
    
    summary = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': summary_prompt}]
    ).choices[0].message.content
    
    # Replace history with summary message
    chat.messages = chat.messages[-3:] + [Message(sender='SUMMARIZER', content=summary)]
    return chat.messages

fixes.name: Smart Speaker Rotation
fixes.description: Enforces balanced participation to prevent context bloat from dominant speakers
fixes.code:
from ag2.groupchat import GroupChat
from collections import deque, Counter

class BalancedRotation:
    def __init__(self):
        self.recent_speakers = deque(maxlen=10)
        self.speaker_quota = Counter()
    
    def select_next_speaker(self, chat: GroupChat, candidates):
        recent_speakers = list(self.recent_speakers)
        
        # Prioritize agents who haven't spoken recently
        eligible = [agent for agent in candidates 
                   if agent.name not in recent_speakers[-3:]]
        
        if eligible:
            selected = min(eligible, key=lambda a: self.speaker_quota[a.name])
        else:
            selected = min(candidates, key=lambda a: self.speaker_quota[a.name])
        
        self.recent_speakers.append(selected.name)
        self.speaker_quota[selected.name] += 1
        
        return selected

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with AG2 through message hooks, automatically monitoring context window usage across all group chats and agent conversations. It detects token overflow, conversation drift, and speaker imbalances in real-time, providing actionable alerts and automated remediation. 

The Vex SDK (`pip install vex-sdk`) registers as an AG2 message observer, tracking metrics like token consumption, semantic drift, and participation balance without modifying core agent logic. When thresholds are breached, Vex can trigger context pruning, summarization, or speaker rotation automatically.

For production deployments, Vex provides dashboards showing context health across all AG2 groups, with one-click remediation workflows and historical analysis to prevent recurrence.
